title: Course Review and Final Quiz
description: Review everything you have learned and test your knowledge with a comprehensive final assessment covering all modules.
objectives: Review key concepts from all modules
Demonstrate knowledge of file management, Word, PDF, and Excel
Apply skills to realistic workplace scenarios
Pass the final assessment with 70% or higher
content:
Congratulations on Reaching the Final Assessment!

You have covered a lot of ground: file management, professional document creation, PDF handling, spreadsheet basics, formulas, and real workplace projects. This final assessment is designed to test your practical knowledge, not just your memory.

Key Concepts to Review

  • Files & Folders: Naming conventions, Save vs Save As, folder structure, backups, housekeeping, archiving.
  • Word: Styles, headers, page numbers, tables, mail merge, professional formatting, templates, memos, reports, watermarks, drop caps, section breaks, find & replace, spell check.
  • PDF: Why PDFs are used, filling forms, combining files, converting from Word/Excel, using free online tools like ilovepdf.com and smallpdf.com.
  • Excel: Headers, currency formatting, sorting, filtering, AutoSum, SUM, SUMIF, AVERAGE, AVERAGEIF, COUNTIF, IF, nested IF, MAX, MIN, charts (column, pie, line).
  • Projects: Letterhead, meeting agenda, mail merge, memos, expense tracker, inventory, business budget, invoice, payroll, income/expenditure statement, grade analysis.

How to Prepare

Go back to any lessons where you struggled with the quiz. Re-read the workplace scenarios. Practice the exercises again. The final quiz covers all modules, so make sure you are confident in each area before starting.

What Happens After

When you pass the final quiz (70% or higher), you will earn the Workplace Ready badge and 50 bonus XP. This is a major milestone in your digital skills journey. Good luck!

downloads.title: Course Review Notes
downloads.description: Summary of all key concepts covered in the course

exercise: Review your notes from all modules. Re-complete any practical exercises you found challenging. When you feel ready, take the final quiz below.

quiz.question: You need to send a final contract to a client. Which file type should you use?
quiz.explanation: PDF preserves formatting and prevents accidental edits, making it ideal for final contracts.

quiz.question: Which Excel formula calculates the total of only 'Travel' expenses in a category column?
quiz.explanation: SUMIF adds values only where the condition (category = 'Travel') is met.

quiz.question: What is the best practice for naming a work file?
quiz.explanation: A descriptive name with date, client, and version makes files easy to find and organize.

quiz.question: Why should you use Word Styles for headings?
options: They ensure consistency and make the document easier to navigate
quiz.explanation: Styles ensure all headings look the same and create a professional, consistent appearance.

quiz.question: An invoice should be sent as a PDF because:
options: PDFs prevent editing and preserve professional formatting
quiz.explanation: PDF is the standard for final documents because it looks the same everywhere and cannot be easily edited.

quiz.question: In Excel, which feature highlights cells that are below a certain number?
quiz.explanation: Conditional Formatting applies visual rules (like red fill) to cells that meet specific criteria.

quiz.question: What does =AVERAGE(B2:B20) calculate?
options: The mean of the numbers in the range
quiz.explanation: AVERAGE calculates the mean (sum divided by count) of all numbers in the specified range.

quiz.question: When combining multiple PDFs into one, what is the most common mistake?
options: Merging in the wrong page order
quiz.explanation: Always check the combined PDF to ensure pages are in the correct logical order, not alphabetical.

quiz.question: Which free online tool can convert a PDF to an editable Word document?
quiz.explanation: ilovepdf.com offers a free PDF to Word converter that creates an editable document from a PDF.

quiz.question: What does =IF(B2>=50, 'PASS', 'FAIL') do?
options: Checks if B2 is 50 or above and returns 'PASS' or 'FAIL'
quiz.explanation: The IF function tests a condition and returns one value if true and another if false.

quiz.question: Why should you use Mail Merge for bulk letters?
options: It saves time and reduces errors when sending personalized letters to many people
It changes the font color automatically
quiz.explanation: Mail Merge automates personalization, saving hours and reducing the risk of typos or wrong names.

quiz.question: What is a memo used for?
quiz.explanation: A memo is a short internal document used to communicate information within an organization.

quiz.question: Which chart type is best for showing parts of a whole?
quiz.explanation: A pie chart shows the proportion of each category as a slice of the whole, ideal for budget allocation.

quiz.question: What is the correct formula for Net Pay?
quiz.explanation: Net Pay is the amount an employee receives after all deductions (tax, NHIF, NSSF) are subtracted from Gross Pay.

quiz.question: Why should you add a gutter margin to a document?
options: To add extra space for binding so text is not hidden
quiz.explanation: A gutter margin adds extra space on the binding side so that text is not obscured when the document is printed and bound.
